title: Piquant Ukrainian Narutomaki Pasta
description: A citrusy, tender pasta dish that blends traditional Ukrainian flavors with Japanese-inspired narutomaki for a romantic, egg‑free dinner.
instructions: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the spaghetti until al dente.
While the pasta is cooking, heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the minced garlic and sauté for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the sliced bell pepper and cherry tomatoes to the skillet and cook for 3 minutes until softened.
Add the narutomaki rounds to the skillet and cook for 2 minutes, letting them warm through.
Stir in the cooked spaghetti, lemon juice, lemon zest, and parsley, tossing to combine.
Season the pasta with salt, pepper, and red pepper flakes to taste.
Serve the pasta hot, garnished with extra parsley if desired.
Enjoy this bright, romantic dish with a glass of chilled white wine.
Store any leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 2 days.
